summary:
The 23-valent pneumococcal vaccine effectively prevents recurrent otitis media and sinusitis by targeting S. pneumoniae; however, it may be most beneficial for individuals with sickle cell disease who face higher risks despite vaccinations due to their compromised immunity.
The 23-valent pneumococcal vaccine reduces episodes of otitis media and sinusitis through its target on S. pneumoniae, yet appears particularly advantageous for people with sickle cell disease because they have an increased susceptibility regardless of immunizations.
